title: Laborers, advocates, and elected officials voiced their support of Senate Bill 37, The Hazardous Waste Site Cleanup and Safety Act , for increased oversight on the cleanup of conta

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Senate Bill 37 aims to close a loophole that allows exemptions to the California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA) for 'Cortese List' sites.",
    "The 'Cortese List' includes sites, often involving underground storage tanks, that pose a risk to public health and safety.",
    "Projects on sites listed on the 'Cortese List' can bypass important CEQA review, jeopardizing public health and worker safety.",
    "Common-Sense exemptions have been granted to projects without adequate environmental review, which has posed significant risks to public health.",
    "SB 37 will prohibit the use of Common-Sense exemptions for projects located on 'Cortese List' sites.",
    "Laborers and building trades workers are exposed to carcinogens and dangerous chemicals at contaminated sites.",
    "There are tragic examples of health risks posed by contaminated sites, such as Bayview-Hunters Point and Treasure Island.",
    "Exposures to toxic substances from contaminated sites can penetrate the soil and groundwater, posing risks to human health and the environment.",
    "For over three decades, the 'Cortese List' has documented contamination to ensure oversight and mitigation for redevelopment sites."
  ]
}
```
